report_name: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: The attack chain begins with an obfuscated batch script (install_obf.bat) which contains and decodes the main Python payload.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The DEEP#DOOR malware attempts to disable Windows security controls, such as Windows Defender, upon execution to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Python
attack_patterns.description: The core implant of DEEP#DOOR (svc.py) is a Python script that functions as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: DEEP#DOOR establishes persistence by creating scripts in the user's Startup folder and adding entries to the Registry Run keys.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: DEEP#DOOR creates a Scheduled Task as one of its redundant methods to maintain persistence on a compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription
attack_patterns.description: DEEP#DOOR uses Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) event subscriptions as a method for persistence.

attack_patterns.name: Protocol Tunneling
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses the legitimate 'bore.pub' tunneling service to create a reverse tunnel for stealthy command and control communications, bypassing network security.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: DEEP#DOOR is designed to steal credentials from web browsers, email clients, and cloud service applications on the victim's machine.

malware.name: DEEP#DOOR
malware.description: A stealthy, multi-stage Python-based backdoor (RAT) that establishes persistence through multiple methods and uses the legitimate 'bore.pub' tunneling service for C2 and data exfiltration, focusing on browser and cloud credentials.

tools.name: bore.pub
tools.description: A legitimate, Rust-based tunneling service abused by DEEP#DOOR for command and control (C2) communications to bypass network security controls.

infrastructure.name: DEEP#DOOR C2 Tunnel
infrastructure.description: C2 infrastructure established by abusing the legitimate bore.pub tunneling service to create a reverse tunnel from the victim machine to the attacker's server.

indicators.name: DEEP#DOOR C2 via bore.pub
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: DEEP#DOOR C2 Communication via bore.pub
id: 9c1b3a2d-8e4f-4b1a-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to bore.pub, a legitimate tunneling service known to be abused by the DEEP#DOOR backdoor for C2 communications.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/new-python-backdoor-uses-tunneling.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-30
tags:
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of the bore.pub tunneling service by developers or tools.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.bore.pub'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the bore.pub tunneling service, which is used by DEEP#DOOR for C2.

indicators.name: DEEP#DOOR File Artifacts
indicators.pattern:
title: DEEP#DOOR Malware File Artifacts
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation or execution of files associated with the DEEP#DOOR backdoor.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/new-python-backdoor-uses-tunneling.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-30
tags:
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1059.006
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_dropper:
    Image|endswith: '\install_obf.bat'
  selection_payload:
    Image|endswith: '\python.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'svc.py'
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the DEEP#DOOR batch dropper or Python payload.

indicators.name: DEEP#DOOR WMI Persistence
indicators.pattern:
title: DEEP#DOOR WMI Event Subscription Persistence
id: f8e7d6c5-b4a3-2109-8765-fedcba987654
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a WMI event subscription, a persistence technique used by DEEP#DOOR. Requires Sysmon Event IDs 19, 20, and 21.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/new-python-backdoor-uses-tunneling.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-30
tags:
  - attack.t1546.003
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: sysmon
detection:
  selection:
    EventID:
      - 19
      - 20
      - 21
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects WMI-based persistence techniques used by DEEP#DOOR.

Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
